messages.content: can u write me this function as a const?

Your code looks mostly good, but there is one small issue in your `handleRegistration` function:

```
database().ref(users/${result.user.uid}).set(userDetails);
```

You forgot to use backticks around the reference path. The correct syntax would be:

```
database().ref(`users/${result.user.uid}`).set(userDetails);
```

Other than that, everything looks good!

database().ref(users/${result.user.uid}).set(userDetails);
it says i have err here as well with {} (it says expexted ,) and . (expected ,)

You need to wrap the reference path string with backticks (`), and there should be quotes (`'`) around the `users/${result.user.uid}` part, like this:

```
database().ref(`users/${result.user.uid}`).set(userDetails);
``` 

Make sure to use the correct syntax with the backticks and quotes, otherwise you'll get a syntax error.

Yes, that looks correct! Since you're using backticks for the path, you need to use `${}` to insert the `result.user.uid` variable into the path. And the `userDetails` object will be set as the data for that database reference.
so now i can use this const?

Yes, your code should work now! Just remember to call `handleRegistration` when the user presses the registration button, like this:

```
<Pressable style={gStyle.RegRegistrBtn} onPress={handleRegistration}>
  <Text style={gStyle.RegRegistration}>Зарегистрироваться</Text>
</Pressable>
``` 

This will trigger the function and attempt to register the user. Let me know if you have any other questions!
oh, before that i have to prebuild my app first, yes?

Before running your app, you need to make sure that you have set up Firebase in your project and added the necessary dependencies to your `package.json`. 

You will also need to import the `Alert` component from `react-native` before you can use it in your Registartion.js file, like this:

```
import { Alert } from 'react-native';
```

Once you have done these things, then you can build and run your app to test the registration feature.

its my package.json

Your `package.json` file looks good. Make sure to run `npm install` to download the necessary dependencies before running your app.

Also, note that you have the `firebase` package as a dependency. However, in your code, you're using the `@react-native-firebase/auth` and `@react-native-firebase/database` packages instead. If you're not planning to use the `firebase` package, you can remove it from your `package.json` file to reduce the size of your app.
do i need to prebuild my app? whats the command for it?

You don't need to prebuild your app before running it. You can run it using the commands in your `package.json` file. 

To run your app on Android, use the command:

```
npm run android
```

To run your app on iOS, use the command:

```
npm run ios
```

To run your app in a web browser, use the command:

```
npm run web
```

Make sure that you have a device or simulator/emulator set up for Android or iOS testing. For more information on running and testing your app, see the Expo documentation: https://docs.expo.io/get-started/running-your-app/
